metadata.name: Docker MySQL Restore
metadata.description: One-shot Bash restore script for importing a .sql or .sql.gz MySQL or MariaDB dump into a running Docker container with preflight checks, confirmation flow, optional drop/recreate, and dry-run support.
metadata.guide:
## Overview
This template renders a standalone `main.sh` restore script for MySQL or MariaDB containers.

## What It Does
- Validates Docker, the target container, archive format, and required client binaries before modifying the database.
- Restores a plain SQL or gzipped SQL archive into either one database or an all-databases style dump.
- Supports interactive confirmation, `--yes` automation, `--dry-run`, and optional drop/recreate for single-database restores.

## Prerequisites
- The target MySQL or MariaDB container must already be running.
- The archive should be a dump generated by `mysqldump` or another compatible plain-SQL workflow.
- Export the password in the configured host environment variable before running when password auth is required.

## Run
1. Render the template and review the default container, archive, database, and safety values.
2. Export the password env var if needed, for example `export MYSQL_PWD='...'`.
3. Run `bash main.sh --help` first, then execute the restore with prompts or `--yes`.

## Notes
- `--drop-recreate` is only valid for a single target database, not for `all` database dumps.
- `--dry-run` prints the actions that would run without modifying the target.

## Docs
- [mysqldump Documentation](https://dev.mysql.com/doc/refman/8.0/en/mysqldump.html)
- [mysql Client Documentation](https://dev.mysql.com/doc/refman/8.0/en/mysql.html)

variables.name: container_name
variables.title: Container Name
variables.description: Name of the running MySQL or MariaDB container.

variables.name: archive_path
variables.title: Archive Path
variables.description: Absolute path to the .sql or .sql.gz dump archive on the Docker host.

variables.name: mysql_user
variables.title: MySQL User
variables.description: Database user used by mysql for the restore.

variables.name: mysql_database
variables.title: Database Name
variables.description: Target database to restore, or 'all' for an all-databases dump.

variables.name: mysql_password_env
variables.title: Password Env Variable
variables.description: Host environment variable that stores the MySQL password.

variables.name: drop_recreate_database
variables.title: Drop/Recreate Database
variables.description: If enabled, drop and recreate the target database before restore.

variables.name: dry_run
variables.title: Dry Run
variables.description: Print planned actions without changing the container.
